steps.title: Set up equation
steps.content: The goal is to find the value of $x$ for which $\sqrt[15]{272n^8} = (272n)^{30x}$. Start by rewriting the left side using exponent rules.

steps.title: Simplify the left side
steps.content: $$\sqrt[15]{272n^8} = (272n^8)^{\frac{1}{15}} = 272^{\frac{8}{15}} n^{\frac{8}{15}} \quad \text{(equating this to the right side)} $$

steps.title: Equate the exponents of n and compare to right side
steps.content:
Now, since $(272^{\frac{8}{15}})(n^{\frac{8}{15}}) = (272n)^{30x}$, focus on equating $n^{\frac{8}{15}}$ to $(n)^{30x}$.
This gives us $\frac{8}{15} = 30x$.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
$$30x = \frac{8}{15}$$
Divide both sides by 30:
$x = \frac{8}{15} * \frac{1}{30}$
Simplifying, we get $x = \frac{8}{450}$ or reducibly to $x=\frac{4}{225}$.
The answer is 4/225.
